Always Check 403 Endpoints: The Hidden Gateway to Your First Critical Bug + Video

Introduction: In the world of web application penetration testing and bug bounty hunting, a "403 Forbidden" error is often seen as a dead end by beginners. However, seasoned security researchers view it as a starting…

external.description: Introduction: In the world of web application penetration testing and bug bounty hunting, a "403 Forbidden" error is often seen as a dead end by beginners. However, seasoned security researchers view it as a starting line. A 403 status code indicates that the server has recognized your request but is refusing to authorize it. This does not necessarily mean the resource is inaccessible; it often means the access control mechanism is poorly implemented.

フロントエンドにおけるキャッシュの全レベルを理解する
この記事では、Webアプリケーションを高速化する様々なキャッシュ技術を、その動作場所と役割ごとに解説しています。
Reactのコンポーネントキャッシュから始まり、実行時のメモリキャッシュ、ブラウザのストレージやService Worker、そしてHTTPキャッシュと、キャッシュのレベルが下がるにつれてスコープが広がり、開発者の制御が小さくなることを説明。
各キャッシュ手法の具体的な実装方法や注意点、使い分けのポイントを提示し、パフォーマンス改善のために適切なキャッシュ戦略を選択するための知識を提供しています。

AIにスクラムの各役割（顧客、プロダクトオーナー、開発者、スクラムマスター）を割り当て、スクラムガイドに沿ったプロセスで開発を進めました。
その結果、AIが自然言語で連携し、成果物を生成する可能性を示す一方で、AIの性格設定やモデルの選択、指示の粒度など、今後の課題も明らかになりました。

29 Ergebnisse

The image you shared is a promotional poster for a demonstration in Leipzig, Germany, scheduled for March 8, 2026 (International Women's Day), at 15:00 Uhr (3:00 PM) at Heinrich-Schütz-Platz.Key elements from the poster:Title/Slogan: "Emanzipation statt Regression" ("Emancipation instead of Regression").
Main message: "Freiheit erkämpfen, autoritäre Räume zerschlagen!" ("Fight for freedom, smash authoritarian spaces!").
Visual symbolism: Stylized kicking figures in the colors of the Israeli flag (blue and white with Star of David) and the Iranian opposition/resistance flag (often green, white, red with a lion-and-sun emblem or similar anti-regime motifs).
An exploding or bursting Iranian regime symbol (stylized version of the Islamic Republic's emblem).

Overall theme: Solidarity with struggles for freedom and against authoritarianism, specifically linking Iran (opposition to the Islamic Republic/theocracy) and Israel (likely in the context of defense against threats or shared anti-authoritarian stance). The imagery suggests support for emancipatory movements in both contexts, rejecting "regression" (e.g., theocratic oppression in Iran or related authoritarian trends).

This appears to be organized by leftist/antifascist or radical left groups, given phrasing like "autoritäre Räume zerschlagen" (smash authoritarian spaces) and the militant tone. It is promoted in circles associated with @antifa_kollektiv7030 (an Antifa-related account on Instagram) and similar networks.The event ties into International Women's Day (March 8), framing women's emancipation and broader liberation struggles against patriarchal/authoritarian structures (e.g., the Iranian regime's oppression of women and dissidents, while expressing solidarity with Israel in a way that contrasts with more common pro-Palestinian leftist positions).Searches indicate this specific call originates from Leipzig-based leftist/antifascist activis…

Why developers using AI are working longer hours

Software engineering was considered the easiest victory of artificial intelligence. Today companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, Microsoft, and Google have released AI products specifically designed for coding. And a survey of nearly 5,000 technology…

external.description: Software engineering was considered the easiest victory of artificial intelligence. Today companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, Microsoft, and Google have released AI products specifically designed for coding. And a survey of nearly 5,000 technology professionals released in a report last year by Google's DevOps Research and Assessment (DORA) team found that 90 percent of respondents said they were using AI at work – with more than 80 percent saying the technology had increased their productivity. “We see a huge number of people who are relying on AI to get their work done, at least to a moderate amount, which is really fascinating,” says Nathan Harvey, who leads the Dora team.
external.title: Why developers using AI are working longer hours

石神井公園（練馬区）
池と緑が広がる公園で、常時開園の広い空間です。園路や池周辺に人が分散しやすく、ぎゅうぎゅう感を避けやすいのが強みです。桜だけ一点勝負というより、景色全体を楽しむ場所として優秀です。

祖師谷公園・仙川沿い（世田谷区）
Go Tokyoでも、仙川沿いのテラスで桜を楽しめると案内されています。都心の超有名スポットより住宅地寄りで、落ち着いた空気で見やすいタイプです。派手な“映え”より、のんびり見たいならかなり良いです。

播磨坂さくら並木（文京区）
文京区公式では約120本の桜、中央部は緑道として整備されています。駅からも近いのに、観光客の“超定番”感はそこまで強くないので、雰囲気の割にまだ狙いやすいです。まつり開催日にぶつかると人は増えるので、平日昼か朝が無難です。

external.description:
Here comes another disappointment for ChatGPT users. As first reported by _Sources_‘ Alex Heath, OpenAI is yet again delaying its “adult mode” for ChatGPT. A company spokesperson told Heath that “we’re pushing out the launch of adult mode so we can focus on work that is a higher priority for more users right now.”

More specifically, OpenAI’s spokesperson said that things like “gains in intelligence, personality improvements, personalization, and making the experience more proactive” were being prioritized instead. However, the company still wants to release an adult mode, but it would “take more time,” according to the company spokesperson.

The reveal of ChatGPT’s adult mode dates back to October, when OpenAI’s CEO, Sam Altman, posted on X that the company would roll out more age-gating as part of its “treat adults like adults” principle, adding that this would include “erotica for verified adults.” Altman originally said this adult mode would be available in December, but an OpenAI exec later said during a December briefing that it would instead debut in the first quarter of 2026.

With Q1 almost coming to a close, we no longer have a timeframe for when ChatGPT’s adult mode will release. However, OpenAI began rolling out its age prediction tool in January, which may go hand-in-hand with the upcoming adult mode.



Source link
external.title: OpenAI is reportedly pushing back the launch of its ‘adult mode’ even further

OpenAI's Head of Robotics Resigns Over Concerns Regarding Pentagon AI Agreement

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Usuarios: It's not clickbait ✅

#openai #pentagon #resignation 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
Caitlin Kalinowski, who led OpenAI's robotics team after joining from Meta in November 2024, announced her resignation on March 7, 2026, citing ethical concerns related to the company's recent agreement with the U.S. Department of Defense. In social media posts, she expressed that while AI plays a vital role in national security, issues such as warrantless surveillance of Americans and fully autonomous lethal weapons required more thorough discussion and safeguards than were provided. She described her departure as a matter of principle rather than personal conflict, maintaining respect for CEO Sam Altman and the team, and later clarified her primary issue was the rushed nature of the announcement without properly defined guardrails, framing it as a governance problem.

The Pentagon deal, announced shortly after failed negotiations with rival Anthropic (which sought strict limits on domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons, leading to its designation as a supply-chain risk), allows OpenAI's technology in classified environments with claimed technical safeguards and red lines against prohibited uses. OpenAI confirmed Kalinowski's exit and reiterated its position that the agreement enables responsible national security applications while upholding boundaries like no domestic surveillance or autonomous weapons, and committed to ongoing dialogue with stakeholders.

The controversy has sparked backlash, contributing to a reported 295% surge in ChatGPT uninstalls and boosting competitor Anthropic's Claude to the top of U.S. App Store free app charts, highlighting reputational risks for OpenAI amid debates over AI's military applications.
external.title: OpenAI's Head of Robotics Resigns Over Concerns Regarding Pentagon AI Agreement

Iranian Americans in Los Angeles Shift from Celebration to Concern Over Potential Chaos in Iran After Khamenei's Death

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Usuarios: It's not clickbait ✅

#iranianamericans #khameneideath #usiranwar 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, Iranian American communities in Los Angeles, especially in Westwood's 'Tehrangeles' area, initially erupted in jubilation with thousands taking to the streets to celebrate the fall of the long-standing regime they fled after the 1979 Islamic Revolution. Many exiles, including those who escaped persecution or war, viewed his death as a positive step toward a freer Iran. However, within days, the mood among some shifted to apprehension as the conflict escalated rapidly. Interviewees like software engineer Arin Saghatelian, who fled Iran as a child, expressed relief at the regime's weakening but worried that U.S. involvement could lead to a prolonged, costly quagmire reminiscent of the Iraq War after Saddam Hussein's 2003 ouster, with risks of sectarian violence, ethnic fragmentation, and chaos far worse due to Iran's complex demographics. Former dissident Roozbeh Farahanipour echoed these fears, citing historical U.S. interventions in Afghanistan and Iraq as cautionary tales of wasted lives and resources without clear exit strategies. The article notes California's large Iranian diaspora—about half of the 600,000 Iranians in the U.S.—with diverse views influenced by generation, religion, and migration timing, and highlights concerns over potential regional instability in the Middle East without a solid U.S. plan for post-regime transition.
external.title: Iranian Americans in Los Angeles Shift from Celebration to Concern Over Potential Chaos in Iran After Khamenei's Death

external.description: Texas’ 23rd Congressional District may become unexpectedly competitive following Rep. Tony Gonzales’ decision to withdraw from his reelection campaign amid a personal scandal. Gonzales’ exit has left Brandon Herrera, a social media personality and gun rights advocate known as “TheAKGuy,” as the Republican nominee. Herrera’s controversial online presence, which includes past Nazi imagery and membership in the Sons of Confederate Veterans, has drawn criticism and could be a vulnerability in the general election. Democrats see an opening in a district historically held by Republicans but recently showing signs of changing demographics, including strong Latino voter turnout in recent primaries. Katy Padilla Stout, the Democratic nominee, could leverage these factors to compete for a seat that Trump won by 17 points in 2024 but that Clinton narrowly carried in 2016. While national Democratic organizations have not yet committed resources to the district, local party leaders are optimistic that Herrera’s controversial record and the broader political environment in Texas, including competitive Senate races and shifting voter trends, could provide a pathway to victory in November. Republicans, however, maintain that the district remains a deep-red area, emphasizing Herrera’s GOP alignment and the long-standing party advantage. The race illustrates the potential impact of candidate controversies and demographic shifts on electoral dynamics in historically conservative districts.
